title: Pan-Seared Poblano Chicken
description: A Japanese-inspired chicken dish featuring pan-seared poblano peppers and a sweet caramelized sauce. This recipe is perfect for a quick and easy dinner.
instructions: 1. Heat 1 teaspoon of sesame oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
2. Add the chicken breasts and cook for 5-6 minutes per side, or until cooked through.
3. Remove the chicken from the skillet and set aside.
4. Add the poblano peppers to the skillet and cook for 3-4 minutes, or until charred.
5. In a small bowl, whisk together the soy sauce, honey, and rice vinegar.
6. Pour the sauce over the poblano peppers and stir to combine.
7. Return the chicken to the skillet and spoon some of the sauce over the top.
8. Cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, or until the sauce is caramelized.
9. Serve the chicken and poblano peppers hot, garnished with sesame seeds if desired.
